title: Who in the family said "we never get ahead" — and why?
subtitle: A journalling set for tracing the origin of the belief that getting ahead isn't available to you.
excerpt: A journal prompt set for exploring the specific person and moment behind the "we never get ahead" belief: who said it, what was happening for them, what they were afraid of, and whether the belief belongs in your future. Five prompts, twenty to thirty minutes.
sourceNotes: Drawn from The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025), Week 2 prompts on breaking generational money patterns.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you can feel the "we never get ahead" belief running but can't trace where it came from.

content.text: A journal set for tracing the "we never get ahead" belief back to the person who held it first. Five prompts. Allow twenty to thirty minutes.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
Where do you sit right now with the idea that getting ahead is possible for you?
Scale of one to ten if that helps. Write the number, then write why.
Have you ever heard "we never get ahead" said out loud in your family?
Name the moment if one comes to mind. Or name the general feeling the words carry.
Who in the family most believed this? What was happening in their life when the belief formed?
Write what you know about their circumstances. What were they afraid of?
Was the belief true for them in the situation they were in?
Separate their circumstances from yours. Is the same thing true for you right now?
What would change in your daily choices if you truly believed ahead was available to you?
Name one specific thing you would do differently.
Drawn from The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025), Week 2, which addresses breaking generational money patterns through structured reflection.
